title: [RELEASED] Getting the Parant Item of a subitem
excerpt:
Hello all. 
If i have the Item ID of a sub item, is there a way to get to the parent item? 
Thanks. 
Yossi

posts.author: YossiT (yossi  turgeman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello all.

If i have the Item ID of a sub item, is there a way to get to the parent item?

Thanks.
Yossi

posts.author: Helen (Helen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @YossiT!

Yes you can-- although I would recommend for you to query the parent item → subitem with the link column method.

This is because while you can use the subitem Id to find the subitem board Id, and then from there query the subitem groups to see their parent item Ids, this method is somewhat sporadic; sometimes the group name will include the parent item Id, and other times it will only include “topics.”

Not to worry-- this is something our developers are aware of and are implementing.

Hope this helps!

-Helen

posts.author: YossiT (yossi  turgeman)
posts.cookedHtml: @Helen Thank you Helen, it worked.

posts.author: AlexSavchuk (Alex Savchuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m glad to hear Helen’s solution worked out for you

Let me know if you need anything else!

-Alex

posts.author: Helen (Helen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @YossiT!

I’m so happy to announce that we recently released a parent_item field when querying your subitems that should resolve this for you.

[RELEASED] `parent_item` Field When Querying Subitems Announcements
:sparkles:Hi everyone, I’m so pleased to announce that we now have a parent_item field for our subitems! As many of you know, before this addition you could query your subitem column values and you could query the subitem column in your parent items to see your subitem IDs, but there was no direct link between the two. ﻿In order to match the relevant subitem column values with the correct parent item them, this required a manual searching mechanism created by individual developers to…

posts.author: YossiT (yossi  turgeman)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks Amazing Helen. Thank you!

posts.author: system (system)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.
